pi_items.source: Task directory analysis: the underlying data file (auth.log) and setup scripts (setup_task.ps1, export_result.ps1) are missing, so the presence of these IPs in the log cannot be definitively verified. However, RFC 5737 confirms these are synthetic/test IPs, contradicting the task's 'real-world' claim.
Matches the explicit directory requirements in the task description and verifier.py logic.
Matches the explicit directory requirements in the task description and verifier.py logic.

privileged_info_summary: Discovered that the task is entirely defective. Critical scripts (setup_task.ps1, export_result.ps1) and the target data file (auth.log) are completely missing from the environment, meaning the verifier will always fail as it relies on the missing export script to produce task_result.json. While the task description claims the data is a 'real-world server authentication log', the expected IPs in the metadata include standard reserved testing/private blocks (e.g. 203.0.113.45, 198.51.100.22, 192.0.2.111), confirming that the intended data for this task is synthetic.
